steps.title: Use complementary acute angles
steps.content: The two acute angles in a right triangle are complementary. The tangent values of complementary acute angles are reciprocals.

steps.title: Complementary angle identity for right triangles
steps.content: In a right triangle, the two acute angles are complementary—they sum to 90°. If one acute angle is θ, the other is 90° − θ. For complementary angles, the tangent of one equals the reciprocal of the tangent of the other.

steps.title: Calculate the tangent of the other angle
steps.content:
We are given that $\tan(\theta) = \frac{\sqrt{5}}{5}$. Using this complementary angle relationship:
$$\tan(90^{\circ} - \theta) = \frac{1}{\tan(\theta)} = \frac{1}{\frac{\sqrt{5}}{5}} = \frac{5}{\sqrt{5}}$$
That confirms D is correct.
